title: Molluscum contagiosum
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Molluscum contagiosum is a viral skin infection that causes raised, pearl-like papules or nodules.
The condition can spread by direct contact or contaminated objects.
Lesions are usually mild and self-limited.
Extensive disease, genital disease, eye involvement, or immunocompromised-host context requires clinician-directed handling.
